configuration.title: T-Slotted Framing Fasteners, Self-Align Ball Nut and M5 Screw, 1.5 in and 40mm Rail

confirmed: internal_thread = M5 (manufacturer-confirmed: spec field 'Fastener Thread Size: M5', 'Fastener Thread Length: 8mm' on 6000N786's own product page)
drop-in / spring-loaded preferred style (manufacturer-confirmed: 'Nut Style: Self-Aligning Spring-Loaded Ball', 'Fastener Installation Type: Drop In')

fits 6575N203 40x40mm four-slot extrusion family (manufacturer-confirmed via rail-height/series match: 6000N786 spec states 'For Rail Height: Single 1 1/2in, 40mm'; 6575N203's own spec independently confirms 'Rail Height: 40mm, Rail Width: 40mm, T-Slot Width: 8mm, T-Slot Depth: 12.2mm' — both parts are McMaster's matched 40mm single-rail T-slot series)
unresolved: 8 mm slot width is NOT printed as a numeric field directly on 6000N786's own spec table (no 'T-Slot Width' field on the fastener page). The fit claim rests on the rail-height/series cross-reference described above, which is standard-derived from McMaster's own paired-series convention, not a literal '8 mm slot' sentence on the nut's page itself. Treat the slot-width match as manufacturer-confirmed-by-series-pairing, one level short of a literal same-page numeric statement. No separate dimensioned 2-D drawing/PDF link could be resolved as a clickable element in this session to provide an even stronger same-document statement (see risks).

evidence.drawing: NOT RETRIEVED — no resolvable 2-D PDF/DWG anchor found on 6000N786's live page in this session (the '2-D PDF' label appearing in page innerText did not correspond to a clickable anchor/button element found via DOM query)

cad_measurements.description: Overall combined screw+nut assembly bounding box
Nut-body cross-section half-width at narrowest sectioned point (y~0, near slot-insertion profile)
cad_measurements.caveat: Plausibility check only — consistent with, but does not independently prove, an 8mm-slot fit. CAD export is a faceted multi-fragment mesh (90 disjoint pieces); could not isolate a clean single nut-body solid.

alternatives.reason: For Rail Height: Single 20mm / Double-Quad 40mm — this is a 20mm-series profile (the '40mm' is the stacked double/quad height, not a single 40x40mm rail). 20mm-series T-slot extrusions standardly use a 6mm slot, not the 8mm slot on 6575N203. Contradicts the hard slot-fit requirement on series grounds.

risks: No direct dimensioned 2-D drawing was retrieved for 6000N786 in this session; evidence is the manufacturer spec table + cross-referenced extrusion spec, not a single combined drawing. If the lead wants a literal same-document '8mm slot' statement, a follow-up retrieval attempt (different DOM probing, or screenshotting the in-page PDF viewer if it renders as a canvas/iframe rather than an anchor) would close this gap.

McMaster's STEP export for this part is a combined screw+nut assembly with a faceted, non-solid-separated mesh — CAD geometry could not be cleanly split into an isolated nut body for an independent dimensional check beyond bounding-box/cross-section reads.

This part ships as a screw+nut SET (not nut-only). If the bracket-foot design needs the M5 screw length/head style to differ from this set's included button-head M5 hex screw, the nut still satisfies the slot/thread requirement but the included screw might be discarded in favor of a separately sourced screw — flag for lead's screw-length decision once bracket-foot stack height is known.

Category facet pages (e.g. t-slotted-framing-nuts with T-Slot-Width=8mm filter) render blank/virtualized headless in this session (per known McMaster scraping limitation) — could not pull a full nut-only product list to confirm 6000N786 is McMaster's single best/only 8mm-slot+M5+drop-in+spring-ball option; it is the strongest of the two warm-start candidates investigated, not exhaustively compared against every nut-only SKU in that category.
recommended_next_action: Lead may treat 6000N786 as the canonical T-slot nut for the idler-bracket-foot/frame-extrusion joint (M5 thread, drop-in spring-loaded ball nut, McMaster's own 40mm single-rail series pairing with 6575N203). If a literal same-document '8mm slot' statement is required before promotion, a follow-up scout pass should attempt the in-page 2-D PDF/DWG viewer specifically (it may render in a canvas/iframe that needs a different capture approach than anchor-href scraping).
